field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The critical condition of liquid chromatography is the transition point of the separation mode between size exclusion and adsorption, in which the retention of a particular polymer species becomes independent of the length of the polymer chain.
field_evidence.critical_component: The PEO block is “invisible” in principle,
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: acetonitrile (HPLC grade) and water (deionized and filtered with 0.45 µm membrane filter)

conditions.critical_condition_basis: The critical condition of liquid chromatography is the transition point of the separation mode between size exclusion and adsorption, in which the retention of a particular polymer species becomes independent of the length of the polymer chain.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, POSTECH, Pohang, 790-784, Korea; Department of Polymer Science and Engineering, Sungkyunkwan University, Suwon, 440-746, Korea
conditions.notes: Critical condition established empirically using PEG standards (Mn: 1500, 3400, 8000) and applied to PEO-PLLA diblock copolymer. The text notes that the critical condition of MPEO (precursor) is slightly different due to end effects, but the PEG critical condition was used for the analysis.
